    "text": "Example 15  Write each of the following statements in its equivalent contrapositive\nform:\n(i)\nIf my car is in the repair shop, then I cannot go to the market.\n(ii)\nIf Karim cannot swim to the fort, then he cannot swim across the river.\nSolution  (i) Let “p : my car is in the repair shop” and “q : I can not go to the market”.\nThen, the given statement in symbolic form is p → q. Therefore, its contrapositive is\ngiven by ~ q → ~ p.\nNow ~ p : My car is not in the repair shop.\nand\n~ q : I can go to the market\nTherefore, the contrapositive of the given statement is\n“If I can go to the market, then my car is not in the repair shop”.\n(ii)\nProceeding on the lines of the solution of (i), the contrapositive of the statement\nin (ii) is",
